118	class SamplingParams(
119	        msgspec.Struct,
120	        omit_defaults=True,  # type: ignore[call-arg]
121	        # required for @cached_property.
122	        dict=True):  # type: ignore[call-arg]
123	    """Sampling parameters for text generation.
124	
125	    Overall, we follow the sampling parameters from the OpenAI text completion
126	    API (https://platform.openai.com/docs/api-reference/completions/create).
127	    In addition, we support beam search, which is not supported by OpenAI.
128	
129	    Args:
130	        n: Number of output sequences to return for the given prompt.
131	        best_of: Number of output sequences that are generated from the prompt.
132	            From these `best_of` sequences, the top `n` sequences are returned.
133	            `best_of` must be greater than or equal to `n`. By default,
134	            `best_of` is set to `n`. Warning, this is only supported in V0.
135	        presence_penalty: Float that penalizes new tokens based on whether they
136	            appear in the generated text so far. Values > 0 encourage the model
137	            to use new tokens, while values < 0 encourage the model to repeat
138	            tokens.
139	        frequency_penalty: Float that penalizes new tokens based on their
140	            frequency in the generated text so far. Values > 0 encourage the
141	            model to use new tokens, while values < 0 encourage the model to
142	            repeat tokens.
143	        repetition_penalty: Float that penalizes new tokens based on whether
144	            they appear in the prompt and the generated text so far. Values > 1
145	            encourage the model to use new tokens, while values < 1 encourage
146	            the model to repeat tokens.
147	        temperature: Float that controls the randomness of the sampling. Lower
148	            values make the model more deterministic, while higher values make
149	            the model more random. Zero means greedy sampling.
150	        top_p: Float that controls the cumulative probability of the top tokens

150	        top_p: Float that controls the cumulative probability of the top tokens
151	            to consider. Must be in (0, 1]. Set to 1 to consider all tokens.
152	        top_k: Integer that controls the number of top tokens to consider. Set
153	            to 0 (or -1) to consider all tokens.
154	        min_p: Float that represents the minimum probability for a token to be
155	            considered, relative to the probability of the most likely token.
156	            Must be in [0, 1]. Set to 0 to disable this.
157	        seed: Random seed to use for the generation.
158	        stop: list of strings that stop the generation when they are generated.
159	            The returned output will not contain the stop strings.
160	        stop_token_ids: list of tokens that stop the generation when they are
161	            generated. The returned output will contain the stop tokens unless
162	            the stop tokens are special tokens.
163	        bad_words: list of words that are not allowed to be generated.
164	            More precisely, only the last token of a corresponding
165	            token sequence is not allowed when the next generated token
166	            can complete the sequence.
167	        include_stop_str_in_output: Whether to include the stop strings in
168	            output text. Defaults to False.
169	        ignore_eos: Whether to ignore the EOS token and continue generating
170	            tokens after the EOS token is generated.
171	        max_tokens: Maximum number of tokens to generate per output sequence.
172	        min_tokens: Minimum number of tokens to generate per output sequence
173	            before EOS or stop_token_ids can be generated
174	        logprobs: Number of log probabilities to return per output token.
175	            When set to None, no probability is returned. If set to a non-None
176	            value, the result includes the log probabilities of the specified
177	            number of most likely tokens, as well as the chosen tokens.
178	            Note that the implementation follows the OpenAI API: The API will
179	            always return the log probability of the sampled token, so there
180	            may be up to `logprobs+1` elements in the response.
181	        prompt_logprobs: Number of log probabilities to return per prompt token.
182	        detokenize: Whether to detokenize the output. Defaults to True.
183	        skip_special_tokens: Whether to skip special tokens in the output.
184	        spaces_between_special_tokens: Whether to add spaces between special
185	            tokens in the output.  Defaults to True.
186	        logits_processors: list of functions that modify logits based on
187	            previously generated tokens, and optionally prompt tokens as
188	            a first argument.
189	        truncate_prompt_tokens: If set to -1, will use the truncation size
190	            supported by the model. If set to an integer k, will use only
191	            the last k tokens from the prompt (i.e., left truncation).
192	            Defaults to None (i.e., no truncation).
193	        guided_decoding: If provided, the engine will construct a guided
194	            decoding logits processor from these parameters. Defaults to None.
195	        logit_bias: If provided, the engine will construct a logits processor
196	            that applies these logit biases. Defaults to None.
197	        allowed_token_ids: If provided, the engine will construct a logits
198	            processor which only retains scores for the given token ids.
199	            Defaults to None.
200	        extra_args: Arbitrary additional args, that can be used by custom
201	            sampling implementations, plugins, etc. Not used by any in-tree
202	            sampling implementations.
203	    """
204	
205	    n: int = 1
206	    best_of: Optional[int] = None
207	    _real_n: Optional[int] = None
208	    presence_penalty: float = 0.0
209	    frequency_penalty: float = 0.0
210	    repetition_penalty: float = 1.0
211	    temperature: float = 1.0
212	    top_p: float = 1.0

214	    min_p: float = 0.0

217	    stop_token_ids: Optional[list[int]] = None
218	    ignore_eos: bool = False
219	    max_tokens: Optional[int] = 16

222	    prompt_logprobs: Optional[int] = None
223	    # NOTE: This parameter is only exposed at the engine level for now.

233	    truncate_prompt_tokens: Optional[Annotated[int, msgspec.Meta(ge=1)]] = None

237	    # They are set in post_init.

239	    _all_stop_token_ids: set[int] = msgspec.field(default_factory=set)

241	    # Fields used to construct logits processors
242	    guided_decoding: Optional[GuidedDecodingParams] = None
243	    logit_bias: Optional[dict[int, float]] = None
244	    allowed_token_ids: Optional[list[int]] = None
245	    extra_args: Optional[dict[str, Any]] = None
246	
247	    # Fields used for bad words
248	    bad_words: Optional[list[str]] = None
249	    _bad_words_token_ids: Optional[list[list[int]]] = None
250	
251	    @staticmethod
252	    def from_optional(
253	        n: Optional[int] = 1,
254	        best_of: Optional[int] = None,
255	        presence_penalty: Optional[float] = 0.0,
256	        frequency_penalty: Optional[float] = 0.0,
257	        repetition_penalty: Optional[float] = 1.0,
258	        temperature: Optional[float] = 1.0,
259	        top_p: Optional[float] = 1.0,
260	        top_k: int = 0,

264	        stop_token_ids: Optional[list[int]] = None,
265	        bad_words: Optional[list[str]] = None,
266	        include_stop_str_in_output: bool = False,

1	# SPDX-License-Identifier: Apache-2.0
2	# SPDX-FileCopyrightText: Copyright contributors to the vLLM project
3	"""Utility methods for model layers."""
4	from typing import Callable, Optional
5	
6	import torch
7	
8	from vllm import _custom_ops as ops
9	from vllm import envs
10	from vllm.platforms import current_platform
11	
12	
13	def get_token_bin_counts_and_mask(
14	    tokens: torch.Tensor,
15	    vocab_size: int,
16	    num_seqs: int,
17	) -> tuple[torch.Tensor, torch.Tensor]:
18	    # Compute the bin counts for the tokens.
19	    # vocab_size + 1 for padding.
20	    bin_counts = torch.zeros((num_seqs, vocab_size + 1),
21	                             dtype=torch.long,
22	                             device=tokens.device)
23	    bin_counts.scatter_add_(1, tokens, torch.ones_like(tokens))
24	    bin_counts = bin_counts[:, :vocab_size]
25	    mask = bin_counts > 0
26	
27	    return bin_counts, mask
28	
29	
30	def apply_penalties(logits: torch.Tensor, prompt_tokens_tensor: torch.Tensor,
31	                    output_tokens_tensor: torch.Tensor,
32	                    presence_penalties: torch.Tensor,
33	                    frequency_penalties: torch.Tensor,
34	                    repetition_penalties: torch.Tensor) -> torch.Tensor:
35	    """
36	    Applies penalties in place to the logits tensor
37	    logits : The input logits tensor of shape [num_seqs, vocab_size]
38	    prompt_tokens_tensor: A tensor containing the prompt tokens. The prompts 
39	        are padded to the maximum prompt length within the batch using 
40	        `vocab_size` as the padding value. The value `vocab_size` is used 
41	        for padding because it does not correspond to any valid token ID 
42	        in the vocabulary.
43	    output_tokens_tensor: The output tokens tensor.
44	    presence_penalties: The presence penalties of shape (num_seqs, )
45	    frequency_penalties: The frequency penalties of shape (num_seqs, )
46	    repetition_penalties: The repetition penalties of shape (num_seqs, )
47	    """
48	    num_seqs, vocab_size = logits.shape
49	    _, prompt_mask = get_token_bin_counts_and_mask(prompt_tokens_tensor,
50	                                                   vocab_size, num_seqs)

66	def default_unquantized_gemm(layer: torch.nn.Module,
67	                             x: torch.Tensor,
68	                             weight: torch.Tensor,
69	                             bias: Optional[torch.Tensor] = None):
70	    return torch.nn.functional.linear(x, weight, bias)
71	
72	
73	def rocm_unquantized_gemm(layer: torch.nn.Module,
74	                          x: torch.Tensor,
75	                          weight: torch.Tensor,
76	                          bias: Optional[torch.Tensor] = None):
77	    from vllm.platforms.rocm import on_gfx9
78	    k = weight.shape[1]
79	    use_skinny = (envs.VLLM_ROCM_USE_SKINNY_GEMM and on_gfx9() and \
80	                    x.dtype in [torch.float16, torch.bfloat16] \
81	                    and k % 8 == 0 and bias is None)
82	
83	    if use_skinny is not True:
84	        return torch.nn.functional.linear(x, weight, bias)
85	
86	    x_view = x.view(-1, x.size(-1))
87	    n = x_view.shape[0]
88	    m = weight.shape[0]
89	    cu_count = current_platform.get_cu_count()
90	
91	    if m > 8 and 0 < n <= 4:
92	        out = ops.wvSplitK(weight, x_view, cu_count)
93	        return out.view(*x.shape[:-1], weight.shape[0])
94	    elif m % 4 == 0 and n == 1 and k <= 8192:
95	        out = ops.LLMM1(weight, x_view, 4)
96	        return out.view(*x.shape[:-1], weight.shape[0])
97	    return torch.nn.functional.linear(x, weight, bias)
98	
99	
100	def cpu_unquantized_gemm(layer: torch.nn.Module,
101	                         x: torch.Tensor,
102	                         weight: torch.Tensor,
103	                         bias: Optional[torch.Tensor] = None):
104	    if getattr(layer, "use_cpu_sgl", False):
105	        return torch.ops._C.weight_packed_linear(x, weight, bias, True)
106	    else:
107	        return torch.nn.functional.linear(x, weight, bias)
108	
109	
110	def dispatch_unquantized_gemm() -> Callable[..., torch.Tensor]:
111	    if current_platform.is_rocm():
112	        return rocm_unquantized_gemm
113	    elif current_platform.is_cpu():
114	        return cpu_unquantized_gemm
115	    else:
116	        return default_unquantized_gemm

225	class MinPLogitsProcessor(LogitsProcessor):
226	
227	    def __init__(self, max_num_reqs: int, pin_memory: bool,
228	                 device: DeviceLikeType):
229	        super().__init__()
230	        self.min_p_count: int = 0
231	
232	        self.min_p_cpu_tensor = torch.zeros((max_num_reqs, ),
233	                                            dtype=torch.float32,
234	                                            device="cpu",
235	                                            pin_memory=pin_memory)
236	        self.min_p_cpu = self.min_p_cpu_tensor.numpy()
237	        # Pre-allocated device tensor
238	        self.min_p_device: torch.Tensor = torch.empty((max_num_reqs, ),
239	                                                      dtype=torch.float32,
240	                                                      device=device)
241	        # Current slice of the device tensor
242	        self.min_p: torch.Tensor = self.min_p_device[:0]
243	
244	    def is_argmax_invariant(self) -> bool:
245	        """Min-p never impacts greedy sampling"""
246	        return True
247	
248	    def get_min_p_by_index(self, index: int) -> float:
249	        return float(self.min_p_cpu[index])
250	
251	    def update_state(self, batch_update: Optional[BatchUpdate]):
252	        if not batch_update:
253	            return
254	
255	        needs_update = False
256	        # Process added requests.
257	        for index, params, _ in batch_update.added:
258	            min_p = params.min_p if isinstance(params, SamplingParams) else 0.0
259	            if self.min_p_cpu[index] != min_p:
260	                needs_update = True
261	                self.min_p_cpu[index] = min_p
262	            if min_p:
263	                self.min_p_count += 1
264	
265	        if self.min_p_count:
266	            # Process removed requests.
267	            needs_update |= bool(batch_update.removed)
268	            for index in batch_update.removed:
269	                if self.min_p_cpu[index]:
270	                    self.min_p_count -= 1
271	
272	            # Process moved requests, unidirectional (a->b) and swap (a<->b)
273	            for adx, bdx, direct in batch_update.moved:
274	                change = (min_p_a :=
275	                          self.min_p_cpu[adx]) != (min_p_b :=
276	                                                   self.min_p_cpu[bdx])
277	                needs_update |= change
278	                if change:
279	                    self.min_p_cpu[bdx] = min_p_a
280	                    if direct == MoveDirectionality.SWAP:
281	                        self.min_p_cpu[adx] = min_p_b
282	
283	        # Update tensors if needed.
284	        size = batch_update.batch_size
285	        if self.min_p_count and (needs_update or self.min_p.shape[0] != size):
286	            self.min_p = self.min_p_device[:size]
287	            self.min_p.copy_(self.min_p_cpu_tensor[:size], non_blocking=True)
288	            self.min_p.unsqueeze_(1)
289	
290	    def apply(self, logits: torch.Tensor) -> torch.Tensor:
291	        if not self.min_p_count:
292	            return logits
293	
294	        # Convert logits to probability distribution
295	        probability_values = torch.nn.functional.softmax(logits, dim=-1)
296	        # Calculate maximum probabilities per sequence
297	        max_probabilities = torch.amax(probability_values,
298	                                       dim=-1,
299	                                       keepdim=True)
300	        # Adjust min_p
301	        adjusted_min_p = max_probabilities.mul_(self.min_p)
302	        # Identify valid tokens using threshold comparison
303	        invalid_token_mask = probability_values < adjusted_min_p
304	        # Apply mask using boolean indexing
305	        logits[invalid_token_mask] = -float('inf')
306	        return logits

33	class OpenAIBaseModel(BaseModel):
34	    # OpenAI API does allow extra fields
35	    model_config = ConfigDict(extra="allow")
36	
37	    # Cache class field names
38	    field_names: ClassVar[Optional[set[str]]] = None
39	
40	    @model_validator(mode="wrap")
41	    @classmethod
42	    def __log_extra_fields__(cls, data, handler):
43	        result = handler(data)
44	        if not isinstance(data, dict):
45	            return result
46	        field_names = cls.field_names
47	        if field_names is None:
48	            # Get all class field names and their potential aliases
49	            field_names = set()
50	            for field_name, field in cls.model_fields.items():
51	                field_names.add(field_name)
52	                if alias := getattr(field, "alias", None):
53	                    field_names.add(alias)
54	            cls.field_names = field_names
55	
56	        # Compare against both field names and aliases
57	        if any(k not in field_names for k in data):
58	            logger.warning(
59	                "The following fields were present in the request "
60	                "but ignored: %s",
61	                data.keys() - field_names,
62	            )
63	        return result

427	def _apply_min_p(
428	    logits: torch.Tensor,
429	    min_p: torch.Tensor,
430	) -> torch.Tensor:
431	    """
432	    Adapted from
433	    https://github.com/oobabooga/text-generation-webui/blob/3146124ec01f02c8fb1650a6517cf1b60b537aaf/modules/sampler_hijack.py#L16C17-L16C17
434	    """
435	    probs = torch.softmax(logits, dim=-1)
436	    top_probs, _ = probs.max(dim=-1, keepdim=True)
437	    scaled_min_p = min_p.unsqueeze_(dim=1) * top_probs
438	    tokens_to_remove = probs < scaled_min_p
439	    logits = logits.masked_fill_(tokens_to_remove, -float("inf"))
440	
441	    return logits

55	def test_stop(model):
56	    """Check that we respect the stop words."""
57	
58	    output = model.generate(PROMPT, SamplingParams(temperature=0))
59	    split_text = output[0].outputs[0].text.split()
60	
61	    STOP_IDX = 5
62	    params = SamplingParams(temperature=0, stop=split_text[STOP_IDX])
63	    output = model.generate(PROMPT, params)
64	    new_split_text = output[0].outputs[0].text.split()
65	
66	    # Output should not contain the stop word.
67	    assert len(new_split_text) == STOP_IDX
68	
69	    params = SamplingParams(temperature=0,
70	                            stop=split_text[STOP_IDX],
71	                            include_stop_str_in_output=True)
72	    output = model.generate(PROMPT, params)
73	    new_split_text = output[0].outputs[0].text.split()
74	
75	    # Output should contain the stop word.
76	    assert len(new_split_text) == STOP_IDX + 1
77

79	def test_stop_token_ids(model):
80	    """Check that we respect the stop token ids."""
81	
82	    output = model.generate(PROMPT, SamplingParams(temperature=0))
83	
84	    stop_token_id_0 = output[0].outputs[0].token_ids[5]
85	    stop_token_id_1 = output[0].outputs[0].token_ids[6]
86	
87	    stop_token_ids = [stop_token_id_1, stop_token_id_0]
88	    params = SamplingParams(temperature=0, stop_token_ids=stop_token_ids)
89	    output = model.generate(PROMPT, params)
90	    assert output[0].outputs[0].token_ids[-1] == stop_token_id_0
91	
92	    stop_token_ids = [stop_token_id_0, stop_token_id_1]
93	    params = SamplingParams(temperature=0, stop_token_ids=stop_token_ids)
94	    output = model.generate(PROMPT, params)
95	    assert output[0].outputs[0].token_ids[-1] == stop_token_id_0

4	from collections.abc import Iterable
5	from functools import lru_cache, partial
6	from typing import Optional, Union
7	
8	import torch
9	
10	from vllm.sampling_params import LogitsProcessor
11	from vllm.transformers_utils.tokenizer import AnyTokenizer
12	
13	
14	class AllowedTokenIdsLogitsProcessor:
15	    """Logits processor for constraining generated tokens to a
16	    specific set of token ids."""
17	
18	    def __init__(self, allowed_ids: Iterable[int]):
19	        self.allowed_ids: Optional[list[int]] = list(allowed_ids)
20	        self.mask: Optional[torch.Tensor] = None
21	
22	    def __call__(self, token_ids: list[int],
23	                 logits: torch.Tensor) -> torch.Tensor:
24	        if self.mask is None:
25	            self.mask = torch.ones((logits.shape[-1], ),
26	                                   dtype=torch.bool,
27	                                   device=logits.device)
28	            self.mask[self.allowed_ids] = False
29	            self.allowed_ids = None
30	        logits.masked_fill_(self.mask, float("-inf"))
31	        return logits
32	
33	
34	@lru_cache(maxsize=32)
35	def _get_allowed_token_ids_logits_processor(
36	    allowed_token_ids: frozenset[int],
37	    vocab_size: int,
38	) -> LogitsProcessor:
39	    if not allowed_token_ids:
40	        raise ValueError("Empty allowed_token_ids provided")
41	    if not all(0 <= tid < vocab_size for tid in allowed_token_ids):
42	        raise ValueError("allowed_token_ids contains "
43	                         "out-of-vocab token id")
44	    return AllowedTokenIdsLogitsProcessor(allowed_token_ids)
45	
46	
47	def logit_bias_logits_processor(
48	    logit_bias: dict[int, float],
49	    token_ids: list[int],
50	    logits: torch.Tensor,
51	) -> torch.Tensor:
52	    for token_id, bias in logit_bias.items():
53	        logits[token_id] += bias
54	    return logits
55	
56	
57	def get_logits_processors(
58	    logit_bias: Optional[Union[dict[int, float], dict[str, float]]],
59	    allowed_token_ids: Optional[list[int]],
60	    tokenizer: AnyTokenizer,
61	) -> list[LogitsProcessor]:
62	    logits_processors: list[LogitsProcessor] = []
63	    if logit_bias:
64	        try:
65	            # Convert token_id to integer
66	            # Clamp the bias between -100 and 100 per OpenAI API spec
67	            clamped_logit_bias: dict[int, float] = {
68	                int(token_id): min(100.0, max(-100.0, bias))
69	                for token_id, bias in logit_bias.items()
70	            }
71	        except ValueError as exc:
72	            raise ValueError(
73	                "Found token_id in logit_bias that is not "
74	                "an integer or string representing an integer") from exc
75	
76	        # Check if token_id is within the vocab size
77	        for token_id, bias in clamped_logit_bias.items():
78	            if token_id < 0 or token_id >= len(tokenizer):
79	                raise ValueError(f"token_id {token_id} in logit_bias contains "
80	                                 "out-of-vocab token id")
81	
82	        logits_processors.append(
83	            partial(logit_bias_logits_processor, clamped_logit_bias))
84	
85	    if allowed_token_ids is not None:
86	        logits_processors.append(
87	            _get_allowed_token_ids_logits_processor(
88	                frozenset(allowed_token_ids), len(tokenizer)))
89	
90	    return logits_processors

4	from typing import Callable, Union
5	
6	import torch
7	
8	from vllm.transformers_utils.tokenizer import AnyTokenizer
9	
10	LogitsProcessor = Union[
11	    Callable[[list[int], torch.Tensor], torch.Tensor],
12	    Callable[[list[int], list[int], torch.Tensor], torch.Tensor],
13	]
14	"""LogitsProcessor is a function that takes a list
15	of previously generated tokens, the logits tensor
16	for the next token and, optionally, prompt tokens as a
17	first argument, and returns a modified tensor of logits
18	to sample from."""
19	
20	
21	def get_bad_words_logits_processors(
22	        bad_words: list[str],
23	        tokenizer: AnyTokenizer) -> list[LogitsProcessor]:
24	    bad_words_ids: list[list[int]] = list()
25	
26	    for bad_word in bad_words:
27	        # To prohibit words both at the beginning
28	        # and in the middle of text
29	        # (related to add_prefix_space tokenizer parameter)
30	        for add_prefix_space in [False, True]:
31	            prefix = " " if add_prefix_space else ""
32	            prompt = prefix + bad_word.lstrip()
33	
34	            prompt_token_ids = tokenizer.encode(text=prompt,
35	                                                add_special_tokens=False)
36	
37	            # If no space at the beginning
38	            # or if prefix space produces a new word token
39	            if (not add_prefix_space) or (
40	                    add_prefix_space
41	                    and prompt_token_ids[0] != bad_words_ids[-1][0]
42	                    and len(prompt_token_ids) == len(bad_words_ids[-1])):
43	                bad_words_ids.append(prompt_token_ids)
44	
45	    return [NoBadWordsLogitsProcessor(bad_words_ids=bad_words_ids)]
46	
47	
48	class NoBadWordsLogitsProcessor:
49	    _SMALLEST_LOGIT = float("-inf")
50	    _NEUTRAL_LOGIT = 0.0
51	
52	    def __init__(self, bad_words_ids: list[list[int]]):
53	        self.bad_words_ids = bad_words_ids
54	        self.word_bias: torch.FloatTensor = None
55	
56	    def __call__(
57	        self,
58	        past_tokens_ids: Union[list[int], tuple[int]],
59	        logits: torch.FloatTensor,
60	    ) -> torch.Tensor:
61	        if self.word_bias is None:
62	            self._init_word_bias(logits=logits)
63	
64	        last_token_bias = torch.zeros_like(logits)
65	
66	        for bad_word_ids in self.bad_words_ids:
67	            if len(bad_word_ids) == 1:  # 1-token words already processed
68	                continue
69	
70	            if len(bad_word_ids) > len(past_tokens_ids) + 1:
71	                continue
72	
73	            prefix_length = len(bad_word_ids) - 1
74	            last_token_id = bad_word_ids[-1]
75	            actual_prefix = past_tokens_ids[-prefix_length:]
76	            expected_prefix = bad_word_ids[:prefix_length]
77	
78	            assert len(actual_prefix) == len(expected_prefix)
79	
80	            is_match = tuple(actual_prefix) == tuple(expected_prefix)
81	            last_token_bias[last_token_id] += (self._SMALLEST_LOGIT if is_match
82	                                               else self._NEUTRAL_LOGIT)
83	
84	        logits = logits + self.word_bias + last_token_bias
85	
86	        return logits
87	
88	    def _init_word_bias(self, logits: torch.FloatTensor) -> None:
89	        # Code based on NoBadWordsLogitsProcessor and SequenceBiasLogitsProcessor  # noqa: E501
90	        # from https://github.com/huggingface/transformers/blob/main/src/transformers/generation/logits_process.py
91	
92	        vocab_size = logits.shape[-1]
93	
94	        self._check_token_ids_bounds(vocab_size=vocab_size)
95	
96	        self.word_bias = torch.zeros((vocab_size, ),
97	                                     dtype=torch.float,
98	                                     device=logits.device)
99	
100	        for bad_word_ids in self.bad_words_ids:
101	            if len(bad_word_ids) == 1:
102	                bad_word_id = bad_word_ids[-1]
103	                self.word_bias[bad_word_id] = self._SMALLEST_LOGIT
104	
105	    def _check_token_ids_bounds(self, vocab_size: int) -> None:
106	        invalid_token_ids = []
107	
108	        for bad_word_ids in self.bad_words_ids:
109	            for token_id in bad_word_ids:
110	                if token_id < 0 or token_id >= vocab_size:
111	                    invalid_token_ids.append(token_id)
112	
113	        if len(invalid_token_ids) > 0:
114	            raise ValueError(
115	                f"The model vocabulary size is {vocab_size},"
116	                f" but the following tokens"
117	                f" were specified as bad: {invalid_token_ids}."
118	                f" All token id values should be integers satisfying:"
119	                f" 0 <= token_id < {vocab_size}.")

Perfect! Now I have enough information. Let me compile the comprehensive report:

## Comprehensive Report: Sampling Parameters in vLLM

Based on my thorough exploration of the vLLM codebase, here's everything you need to know about how sampling parameters are defined and how `min_p` specifically was integrated:

---

### 1. **Main SamplingParams Class Definition**

**File:** `/vllm/sampling_params.py` (Lines 118-589)

**Structure:** Uses `msgspec.Struct` with `omit_defaults=True` and `dict=True` flags

**Key fields and their defaults:**
- `n: int = 1` - Number of output sequences
- `best_of: Optional[int] = None` - Number of sequences to generate before selecting top n
- `temperature: float = 1.0` - Randomness control (0 = greedy)
- `top_p: float = 1.0` - Cumulative probability threshold, must be in (0, 1]
- `top_k: int = 0` - Number of top tokens (0 or -1 disables)
- `min_p: float = 0.0` - **Minimum probability relative to max token probability, must be in [0, 1]**
- `presence_penalty: float = 0.0` - Penalties based on token appearance ([-2, 2])
- `frequency_penalty: float = 0.0` - Penalties based on frequency ([-2, 2])
- `repetition_penalty: float = 1.0` - Penalties across prompt and output (> 0)
- `seed: Optional[int] = None` - Random seed
- `stop: Optional[Union[str, list[str]]] = None`
- `stop_token_ids: Optional[list[int]] = None`
- `max_tokens: Optional[int] = 16`
- `min_tokens: int = 0`
- `logprobs: Optional[int] = None`
- `prompt_logprobs: Optional[int] = None`
- `ignore_eos: bool = False`
- `skip_special_tokens: bool = True`
- `detokenize: bool = True`
- `spaces_between_special_tokens: bool = True`
- `logits_processors: Optional[Any] = None`
- `truncate_prompt_tokens: Optional[int] = None`
- `output_kind: RequestOutputKind = RequestOutputKind.CUMULATIVE`
- `guided_decoding: Optional[GuidedDecodingParams] = None`
- `logit_bias: Optional[dict[int, float]] = None`
- `allowed_token_ids: Optional[list[int]] = None`
- `bad_words: Optional[list[str]] = None`
- `extra_args: Optional[dict[str, Any]] = None`

**Internal fields (set in post_init):**
- `_real_n: Optional[int] = None`
- `output_text_buffer_length: int = 0`
- `_all_stop_token_ids: set[int] = msgspec.field(default_factory=set)`
- `_bad_words_token_ids: Optional[list[list[int]]] = None`

---

### 2. **Validation & Post-Init Logic** (Lines 327-386)

The `__post_init__` method:
- Handles `best_of` logic (stores original `n` in `_real_n` if best_of is set)
- Clamps temperature to minimum `_MAX_TEMP = 1e-2` if too low
- Converts seed of -1 to None
- Normalizes stop strings and bad words to lists
- **For greedy sampling (temperature < _SAMPLING_EPS = 1e-5)**, it resets:
  ```python
  self.top_p = 1.0
  self.top_k = 0
  self.min_p = 0.0
  ```
- Accumulates stop token IDs

**Validation method** `_verify_args()` (Lines 387-462):
- `min_p` validated: `if not 0.0 <= self.min_p <= 1.0` (Line 426-428)
- Other parameter ranges checked per their documentation
- Complex constraint validation (e.g., best_of >= n, min_tokens <= max_tokens)

---

### 3. **Sampling Metadata Layer (GPU/CPU Side)**

**File:** `/vllm/model_executor/sampling_metadata.py`

#### SamplingTensors Dataclass (Lines 372-597)
Converts per-sequence sampling params to batched tensors:
- `temperatures: torch.Tensor`
- `top_ps: torch.Tensor`
- `top_ks: torch.Tensor`
- **`min_ps: torch.Tensor`** (Lines 379, 399, 417, 446, 457, 551-556, 588)
- `presence_penalties: torch.Tensor`
- `frequency_penalties: torch.Tensor`
- `repetition_penalties: torch.Tensor`
- `prompt_tokens: torch.Tensor`
- `output_tokens: torch.Tensor`

**Key method:** `from_sampling_metadata()` (Lines 386-495)
- Extracts `min_p` from each seq_group: `min_p = sampling_params.min_p` (Line 416)
- Sets `do_min_p = True` if any request has `min_p > _SAMPLING_EPS` (Lines 429-430)
- Builds `min_ps` list by repeating min_p value for each token in batch
- Converts to tensor at line 551-556 with device transfer and non-blocking copy

---

### 4. **V1 GPU Input Batch (GPU-side Worker)**

**File:** `/vllm/v1/worker/gpu_input_batch.py`

**Note:** Does NOT currently have min_p tensor buffers (unlike TPU version below)

Sampling parameters stored as:
- `temperature`, `temperature_cpu_tensor`, `temperature_cpu`, `greedy_reqs`
- `top_p`, `top_p_cpu_tensor`, `top_p_cpu`, `top_p_reqs`
- `top_k`, `top_k_cpu_tensor`, `top_k_cpu`, `top_k_reqs`
- No min_p fields yet

**Request addition logic** (Lines 266-385):
- Sets temperature, top_p, top_k in CPU arrays when `sampling_params` provided
- Does not handle min_p yet

---

### 5. **V1 TPU Input Batch (TPU-side Worker)**

**File:** `/vllm/v1/worker/tpu_input_batch.py`

**Structure includes min_p fully:**
```python
self.min_p = torch.empty((max_num_reqs, ), dtype=torch.float32, device=device)
self.min_p_cpu_tensor = torch.empty((max_num_reqs, ), dtype=torch.float32, device="cpu", pin_memory=pin_memory)
self.min_p_cpu = self.min_p_cpu_tensor.numpy()
self.min_p_reqs: set[str] = set()
```

**Default sampling params dict** (Lines 10-19):
```python
DEFAULT_SAMPLING_PARAMS = dict(
    temperature=-1.0,
    min_p=0.0,  # <-- Min_p default
    top_k=0,
    top_p=1.0,
)
```

**TPU metadata building** (Lines 99-124):
- Fills min_p with defaults then actual values (Line 105-106)
- Copies to device tensors (Line 122-123)

---

### 6. **V1 TPU Supported Sampling Metadata Dataclass**

**File:** `/vllm/v1/sample/tpu/metadata.py`

**Structure:**
```python
@dataclass
class TPUSupportedSamplingMetadata:
    temperature: torch.Tensor = None
    min_p: torch.Tensor = None      # <-- Explicit field
    top_k: torch.Tensor = None
    top_p: torch.Tensor = None
    all_greedy: bool = True
    # ... other fields
```

---

### 7. **V1 Logits Processor - MinPLogitsProcessor**

**File:** `/vllm/v1/sample/logits_processor.py` (Lines 225-307)

**Purpose:** Applies min_p filtering on the GPU

**Key components:**
```python
class MinPLogitsProcessor(LogitsProcessor):
    def __init__(self, max_num_reqs, pin_memory, device):
        self.min_p_count: int = 0
        self.min_p_cpu_tensor = torch.zeros((max_num_reqs,), dtype=torch.float32, ...)
        self.min_p_cpu = self.min_p_cpu_tensor.numpy()
        self.min_p_device: torch.Tensor = torch.empty((max_num_reqs,), ...)
        self.min_p: torch.Tensor = self.min_p_device[:0]
```

**Update logic** (Lines 251-288):
- Tracks which requests have non-zero min_p via `min_p_count`
- Handles request addition/removal/movement in batch
- Only updates tensors if `min_p_count > 0` (optimization)
- Copies min_p values to device and unsqueezes for broadcasting

**Application logic** (Lines 290-306):
```python
def apply(self, logits: torch.Tensor) -> torch.Tensor:
    probability_values = torch.nn.functional.softmax(logits, dim=-1)
    max_probabilities = torch.amax(probability_values, dim=-1, keepdim=True)
    adjusted_min_p = max_probabilities.mul_(self.min_p)
    invalid_token_mask = probability_values < adjusted_min_p
    logits[invalid_token_mask] = -float('inf')
    return logits
```

**Key property:** `is_argmax_invariant() = True` (Line 245) - min_p never affects greedy sampling

---

### 8. **GPU-side Sampler Implementation**

**File:** `/vllm/model_executor/layers/sampler.py`

**Forward pass** (Lines 225-290):
- Initializes `SamplingTensors` which includes min_ps
- Tracks `do_min_p` flag (True if any request has min_p > epsilon)
- Applies transformations in order:
  1. Apply penalties (if needed)
  2. Apply temperature scaling
  3. Apply top-k/top-p filtering
  4. **Apply min_p filtering** (Line 288-289)
  5. Compute probabilities and logprobs
  6. Sample tokens

**`_apply_min_p()` function** (Lines 427-441):
```python
def _apply_min_p(logits: torch.Tensor, min_p: torch.Tensor) -> torch.Tensor:
    probs = torch.softmax(logits, dim=-1)
    top_probs, _ = probs.max(dim=-1, keepdim=True)
    scaled_min_p = min_p.unsqueeze_(dim=1) * top_probs
    tokens_to_remove = probs < scaled_min_p
    logits = logits.masked_fill_(tokens_to_remove, -float("inf"))
    return logits
```

**Attribution:** References text-generation-webui implementation

---

### 9. **How Min_P is Threaded Through (Pattern Reference)**

**Step-by-step flow:**

1. **User API** → `SamplingParams(min_p=0.05)` at `/vllm/sampling_params.py:214`

2. **Validation** → `_verify_args()` checks `0.0 <= min_p <= 1.0` at line 426-428

3. **Post-init** → If greedy (temp < eps), reset to 0.0 at line 381

4. **Metadata preparation** → `SamplingMetadata.prepare()`:
   - Calls `SamplingTensors.from_sampling_metadata()`
   - Extracts min_p from each seq_group (line 416)
   - Sets `do_min_p` flag if needed (lines 429-430)
   - Builds `min_ps` list and converts to tensor (lines 399, 551-556)

5. **Worker batching**:
   - **GPU V0:** Already uses SamplingTensors
   - **GPU V1:** Does not yet have min_p support in `gpu_input_batch.py` (gap!)
   - **TPU V1:** Has full support in `tpu_input_batch.py` with CPU/device tensors

6. **GPU Execution**:
   - Sampler receives `SamplingTensors` with `min_ps` tensor
   - Calls `_apply_min_p(logits, min_ps)` if `do_min_p=True`
   - Masks out tokens below threshold

7. **V1 Logits Processing (Alternative)**:
   - `MinPLogitsProcessor` receives min_p values in `update_state()`
   - Caches in CPU array, copies to device tensor
   - `apply()` method filters logits same algorithm as GPU sampler

---

### 10. **Key Files Summary**

| File Path | Purpose | Min_P Support |
|-----------|---------|---------------|
| `/vllm/sampling_params.py` | Main params class | Full (def=0.0, [0,1], validated) |
| `/vllm/model_executor/sampling_metadata.py` | GPU metadata | Full (tensors) |
| `/vllm/model_executor/layers/sampler.py` | GPU sampling kernel | Full (_apply_min_p) |
| `/vllm/v1/sample/logits_processor.py` | V1 logits processing | Full (MinPLogitsProcessor) |
| `/vllm/v1/sample/metadata.py` | V1 base metadata | Partial (no min_p field) |
| `/vllm/v1/sample/tpu/metadata.py` | TPU metadata | Full (tensors, defaults) |
| `/vllm/v1/worker/gpu_input_batch.py` | V1 GPU batch | **MISSING** min_p tensors |
| `/vllm/v1/worker/tpu_input_batch.py` | V1 TPU batch | Full (tensors, cpu arrays) |
| `/tests/test_sampling_params.py` | Param tests | Basic |
| `/tests/v1/sample/test_sampling_params_e2e.py` | E2E tests | Uses min_p (line 48) |

---

### 11. **Data Types & Shapes**

- **CPU storage:** `torch.float32`
- **GPU storage:** `torch.float32` (default) or matching sampler's dtype
- **Shape for batch:** `(batch_size,)` → unsqueezed to `(batch_size, 1)` for broadcasting
- **Pin memory:** True when available for fast H2D transfer

---

### 12. **Relationship to Similar Parameters**

| Parameter | Type | Range | Default | Algorithm |
|-----------|------|-------|---------|-----------|
| `temperature` | float | [0, ∞) | 1.0 | Scales logits by 1/T |
| `top_p` | float | (0, 1] | 1.0 | Cumulative probability cutoff |
| `top_k` | int | ≥0 | 0 | Token count cutoff |
| `min_p` | float | [0, 1] | 0.0 | **Relative to max prob: filters if p < max_p * min_p** |

The `min_p` implementation follows the same pattern as `top_p` and `top_k` but with a **relative** (multiplicative) threshold rather than absolute.

---

### 13. **Test Coverage**

**Line 48 in `/tests/v1/sample/test_sampling_params_e2e.py`:**
```python
params = SamplingParams(
    temperature=1.2,
    presence_penalty=1.2,
    frequency_penalty=1.2,
    repetition_penalty=1.2,
    min_p=0.5,  # <-- Min_p usage example
    top_p=0.5,
    top_k=3,
)
```

---

This comprehensive overview shows that `min_p` is fully integrated into the sampling pipeline at the CPU/metadata level and GPU execution level, with a parallel V1 implementation using logits processors. The algorithm is consistent across both pathways and follows best practices from the text-generation-webui project.

```python
def _apply_min_p(logits: torch.Tensor, min_p: torch.Tensor) -> torch.Tensor:
    probs = torch.softmax(logits, dim=-1)
    top_probs, _ = probs.max(dim=-1, keepdim=True)
    scaled_min_p = min_p.unsqueeze_(dim=1) * top_probs
    tokens_to_remove = probs < scaled_min_p
    logits = logits.masked_fill_(tokens_to_remove, -float("inf"))
    return logits
```

**Attribution:** References text-generation-webui implementation

---

### 9. **How Min_P is Threaded Through (Pattern Reference)**

**Step-by-step flow:**

1. **User API** → `SamplingParams(min_p=0.05)` at `/vllm/sampling_params.py:214`

2. **Validation** → `_verify_args()` checks `0.0 <= min_p <= 1.0` at line 426-428

3. **Post-init** → If greedy (temp < eps), reset to 0.0 at line 381

4. **Metadata preparation** → `SamplingMetadata.prepare()`:
   - Calls `SamplingTensors.from_sampling_metadata()`
   - Extracts min_p from each seq_group (line 416)
   - Sets `do_min_p` flag if needed (lines 429-430)
   - Builds `min_ps` list and converts to tensor (lines 399, 551-556)

5. **Worker batching**:
   - **GPU V0:** Already uses SamplingTensors
   - **GPU V1:** Does not yet have min_p support in `gpu_input_batch.py` (gap!)
   - **TPU V1:** Has full support in `tpu_input_batch.py` with CPU/device tensors

6. **GPU Execution**:
   - Sampler receives `SamplingTensors` with `min_ps` tensor
   - Calls `_apply_min_p(logits, min_ps)` if `do_min_p=True`
   - Masks out tokens below threshold

7. **V1 Logits Processing (Alternative)**:
   - `MinPLogitsProcessor` receives min_p values in `update_state()`
   - Caches in CPU array, copies to device tensor
   - `apply()` method filters logits same algorithm as GPU sampler

---

### 10. **Key Files Summary**

| File Path | Purpose | Min_P Support |
|-----------|---------|---------------|
| `/vllm/sampling_params.py` | Main params class | Full (def=0.0, [0,1], validated) |
| `/vllm/model_executor/sampling_metadata.py` | GPU metadata | Full (tensors) |
| `/vllm/model_executor/layers/sampler.py` | GPU sampling kernel | Full (_apply_min_p) |
| `/vllm/v1/sample/logits_processor.py` | V1 logits processing | Full (MinPLogitsProcessor) |
| `/vllm/v1/sample/metadata.py` | V1 base metadata | Partial (no min_p field) |
| `/vllm/v1/sample/tpu/metadata.py` | TPU metadata | Full (tensors, defaults) |
| `/vllm/v1/worker/gpu_input_batch.py` | V1 GPU batch | **MISSING** min_p tensors |
| `/vllm/v1/worker/tpu_input_batch.py` | V1 TPU batch | Full (tensors, cpu arrays) |
| `/tests/test_sampling_params.py` | Param tests | Basic |
| `/tests/v1/sample/test_sampling_params_e2e.py` | E2E tests | Uses min_p (line 48) |

---

### 11. **Data Types & Shapes**

- **CPU storage:** `torch.float32`
- **GPU storage:** `torch.float32` (default) or matching sampler's dtype
- **Shape for batch:** `(batch_size,)` → unsqueezed to `(batch_size, 1)` for broadcasting
- **Pin memory:** True when available for fast H2D transfer

---

### 12. **Relationship to Similar Parameters**

| Parameter | Type | Range | Default | Algorithm |
|-----------|------|-------|---------|-----------|
| `temperature` | float | [0, ∞) | 1.0 | Scales logits by 1/T |
| `top_p` | float | (0, 1] | 1.0 | Cumulative probability cutoff |
| `top_k` | int | ≥0 | 0 | Token count cutoff |
| `min_p` | float | [0, 1] | 0.0 | **Relative to max prob: filters if p < max_p * min_p** |

The `min_p` implementation follows the same pattern as `top_p` and `top_k` but with a **relative** (multiplicative) threshold rather than absolute.

---

### 13. **Test Coverage**

**Line 48 in `/tests/v1/sample/test_sampling_params_e2e.py`:**

This comprehensive overview shows that `min_p` is fully integrated into the sampling pipeline at the CPU/metadata level and GPU execution level, with a parallel V1 implementation using logits processors. The algorithm is consistent across both pathways and follows best practices from the text-generation-webui project.

I've conducted a thorough exploration of the sampling and filtering mechanisms in vLLM. Here's what I found:

## 1. SAMPLING PARAMETERS & CONFIGURATION

**File:** `/vllm/sampling_params.py` (lines 1-603)

Key sampling parameters:
- **top_p** (line 212): Float controlling cumulative probability of top tokens, range (0, 1]
- **top_k** (line 213): Integer controlling number of top tokens to consider, range [0, ∞)
- **min_p** (line 214): Float representing minimum probability relative to max token, range [0, 1]
- **temperature** (line 211): Controls randomness; zero means greedy sampling
- **presence_penalty, frequency_penalty, repetition_penalty** (lines 208-210)

Validation logic at lines 387-428 ensures all params are valid before use.

---

## 2. METADATA STRUCTURES - REQUEST TO SAMPLING FLOW

**File:** `/vllm/model_executor/sampling_metadata.py` (lines 1-598)

### A. SamplingParams → SequenceGroupToSample (lines 19-76)
```
Request (with SamplingParams)
  ↓
SequenceGroupToSample (dataclass containing):
  - seq_ids: list[int]
  - sampling_params: SamplingParams
  - seq_data: dict[int, SequenceData]
  - seq_len, query_len, generator
  - is_prompt: bool
  - prompt_logprob_indices, sample_indices: list[int]
```

### B. SamplingMetadata (lines 97-196)
Main metadata structure passed to sampler:
- `seq_groups: list[SequenceGroupToSample]` - batched sequences
- `selected_token_indices: torch.Tensor` - indices to extract logits from model output
- `categorized_sample_indices: dict[SamplingType, torch.Tensor]` - indices per sampling type
- `num_prompts: int`

### C. SamplingTensors (lines 372-597)
Tensors extracted from SamplingParams for GPU:
- `temperatures: torch.Tensor` - per-sequence temperature
- `top_ps: torch.Tensor` - per-sequence top-p values
- `top_ks: torch.Tensor` - per-sequence top-k values
- **`min_ps: torch.Tensor`** - per-sequence min-p values (line 379)
- `presence_penalties, frequency_penalties, repetition_penalties: torch.Tensor`
- `prompt_tokens, output_tokens: torch.Tensor` - for penalty calculation

**Construction method:** `SamplingTensors.from_sampling_metadata()` (lines 386-495)
- Extracts sampling params from each seq_group
- Detects if top-p/top-k is needed (lines 426-430)
- Detects if min-p is needed (line 429): `do_min_p = min_p > _SAMPLING_EPS`
- Creates per-sequence tensors pinned in CPU memory for async H2D transfer

---

## 3. CORE FILTERING LOGIC - V0 (Legacy) Sampler

**File:** `/vllm/model_executor/layers/sampler.py` (lines 1-1500+)

### A. Main Sampler Forward Pass (lines 225-334)
```python
def forward(logits, sampling_metadata):
    logits = _apply_min_tokens_penalty(logits, sampling_metadata)  # Line 269
    
    # 1. Apply penalties (presence, frequency, repetition)
    if do_penalties:
        logits = apply_penalties(logits, ...)  # Lines 272-277
    
    # 2. Temperature scaling
    logits.div_(temperatures)  # Line 282
    
    # 3. TOP-K/TOP-P filtering
    if do_top_p_top_k and flashinfer_sampling is None:
        logits = _apply_top_k_top_p(logits, top_ps, top_ks)  # Lines 284-286
    
    # 4. MIN-P filtering
    if do_min_p:
        logits = _apply_min_p(logits, min_ps)  # Lines 288-289
    
    # 5. Compute probabilities
    probs = softmax(logits)  # Line 293
    logprobs = log_softmax(logits)  # Line 295
    
    # 6. Sample from distribution
    sample_results = _sample(probs, logprobs, ...)  # Lines 298-305
```

### B. Top-K/Top-P Filtering (lines 398-424)

**Algorithm:**
1. Sort logits in descending order (line 403)
2. **Top-K:** Keep only top-k values, set others to -∞ (lines 406-410)
3. **Top-P:** Keep tokens until cumulative probability ≤ 1-p (lines 413-418)
4. Re-scatter back to original positions (lines 421-423)

```python
def _apply_top_k_top_p(logits, p, k):
    logits_sort, logits_idx = logits.sort(dim=-1, descending=False)
    
    # Top-k mask
    top_k_mask = logits_sort.size(1) - k.to(torch.long)
    top_k_mask = logits_sort.gather(1, top_k_mask.unsqueeze(dim=1))
    top_k_mask = logits_sort < top_k_mask
    logits_sort.masked_fill_(top_k_mask, -float("inf"))
    
    # Top-p mask (on softmaxed probs)
    probs_sort = logits_sort.softmax(dim=-1)
    probs_sum = probs_sort.cumsum(dim=-1)
    top_p_mask = probs_sum <= 1 - p.unsqueeze(dim=1)
    top_p_mask[:, -1] = False  # Ensure at least 1 token
    logits_sort.masked_fill_(top_p_mask, -float("inf"))
    
    # Scatter back
    logits = torch.empty_like(logits_sort).scatter_(
        dim=-1, index=logits_idx, src=logits_sort)
    return logits
```

### C. Min-P Filtering (lines 427-441)

**Algorithm:** Filter tokens below `min_p * max_probability`

```python
def _apply_min_p(logits, min_p):
    probs = torch.softmax(logits, dim=-1)
    top_probs, _ = probs.max(dim=-1, keepdim=True)
    scaled_min_p = min_p.unsqueeze_(dim=1) * top_probs  # Scale by max prob
    tokens_to_remove = probs < scaled_min_p
    logits = logits.masked_fill_(tokens_to_remove, -float("inf"))
    return logits
```

**Key insight:** min_p is relative to the maximum probability in the distribution.

### D. Penalty Application (lines 351-395)

Via `apply_penalties()` in `/vllm/model_executor/layers/utils.py` (lines 30-63):
- Computes binary masks for prompt/output tokens
- Applies repetition penalty via CUDA kernel `apply_repetition_penalties`
- Applies frequency penalty: `logits -= frequency_penalties * output_bin_counts`
- Applies presence penalty: `logits -= presence_penalties * output_mask`

---

## 4. V1 (NEW) SAMPLER WITH FLASHINFER INTEGRATION

**File:** `/vllm/v1/sample/sampler.py` (lines 1-200+)

### A. V1 Forward Pass (lines 24-76)

More modular design with separate concerns:

```python
def forward(logits, sampling_metadata):
    # 1. Allowed token filtering
    logits = apply_allowed_token_ids(logits, metadata)
    
    # 2. Bad words exclusion
    logits = apply_bad_words(logits, metadata)
    
    # 3. Non-argmax-invariant processors
    for processor in logitsprocs.non_argmax_invariant:
        logits = processor.apply(logits)
    
    # 4. Penalties (min_tokens, freq, presence, repetition)
    logits = apply_penalties(logits, metadata)
    
    # 5. Sample (handles temperature, top-k, top-p internally)
    sampled = sample(logits, metadata)
    
    # 6. Compute logprobs if requested
    logprobs_tensors = gather_logprobs(raw_logprobs, ...)
```

### B. V1 SamplingMetadata (lines 1-44 in `/vllm/v1/sample/metadata.py`)

```python

    temperature: Optional[torch.Tensor]
    all_greedy: bool

```

---

## 5. TOP-K/TOP-P IMPLEMENTATIONS - V1

**File:** `/vllm/v1/sample/ops/topk_topp_sampler.py` (lines 1-297)

### A. TopKTopPSampler Class (lines 22-119)

**Three implementations based on platform:**

1. **CUDA + FlashInfer (lines 85-107):** `forward_cuda()`
   - Uses FlashInfer library for optimized GPU sampling
   - Falls back to native if generators provided (per-request seeds)
   
2. **CPU/Native PyTorch (lines 69-83):** `forward_native()`
   - Pure PyTorch implementation
   
3. **TPU (lines 109-118):** `forward_tpu()`
   - TPU-optimized algorithm avoiding scatter operations

### B. Native Top-K/Top-P (lines 168-208)

```python
def apply_top_k_top_p(logits, k, p):
    logits_sort, logits_idx = logits.sort(dim=-1, descending=False)
    
    if k is not None:
        top_k_mask = logits_sort.size(1) - k.to(torch.long)
        top_k_mask = logits_sort.gather(1, top_k_mask.unsqueeze(dim=1))
        top_k_mask = logits_sort < top_k_mask
        logits_sort.masked_fill_(top_k_mask, -float("inf"))
    
    if p is not None:
        probs_sort = logits_sort.softmax(dim=-1)
        probs_sum = torch.cumsum(probs_sort, dim=-1, out=probs_sort)
        top_p_mask = probs_sum <= 1 - p.unsqueeze(dim=1)
        top_p_mask[:, -1] = False  # At least one token
        logits_sort.masked_fill_(top_p_mask, -float("inf"))
    
    logits = logits_sort.scatter(dim=-1, index=logits_idx, src=logits_sort)
    return logits
```

### C. Top-K Only Optimization (lines 211-233)

For top-k without top-p, avoids full sort:

```python
def apply_top_k_only(logits, k):
    no_top_k_mask = k == logits.shape[1]
    k = k.masked_fill(no_top_k_mask, 1)
    max_top_k = k.max()
    k_index = k.sub_(1).unsqueeze(1)
    top_k_mask = logits.topk(max_top_k, dim=1).values.gather(1, k_index.long())
    top_k_mask.masked_fill_(no_top_k_mask.unsqueeze(1), -float("inf"))
    logits.masked_fill_(logits < top_k_mask, -float("inf"))
    return logits
```

### D. TPU-Optimized Top-K/Top-P (lines 121-165)

Uses cumulative probability thresholding without scatter:

```python
def apply_top_k_top_p_tpu(logits, k, p):
    probs = logits.softmax(dim=-1)
    probs_sort, _ = probs.sort(dim=-1, descending=False)
    
    if k is not None:
        top_k_count = probs_sort.size(1) - k.to(torch.long)
        top_k_count = top_k_count.unsqueeze(dim=1)
        top_k_cutoff = probs_sort.gather(-1, top_k_count)
        no_top_k_mask = (k == logits.shape[1]).unsqueeze(dim=1)
        top_k_cutoff.masked_fill_(no_top_k_mask, -float("inf"))
        elements_to_discard = probs < top_k_cutoff
        logits.masked_fill_(elements_to_discard, -float("inf"))
    
    if p is not None:
        cumprob = torch.cumsum(probs_sort, dim=-1)
        top_p_mask = cumprob <= 1 - p.unsqueeze(dim=1)
        top_p_mask[:, -1] = False
        top_p_count = top_p_mask.sum(dim=-1).unsqueeze(1)
        top_p_cutoff = probs_sort.gather(-1, top_p_count)
        elements_to_discard = probs < top_p_cutoff
        logits.masked_fill_(elements_to_discard, -float("inf"))
    
    return logits
```

### E. Random Sampling (lines 236-257)

Uses exponential variate trick to avoid CPU-GPU sync:

```python
def random_sample(probs, generators):
    q = torch.empty_like(probs)
    if len(generators) != probs.shape[0]:
        q.exponential_()  # All requests without seeds
    if generators:
        for i, generator in generators.items():
            q[i].exponential_(generator=generator)  # Per-request seeds
    return probs.div_(q).argmax(dim=-1).view(-1)
```

### F. FlashInfer Integration (lines 260-296)

```python
def flashinfer_sample(logits, k, p, generators):
    # Uses FlashInfer CUDA kernels for fast sampling
    if k is None:
        probs = logits.softmax(dim=-1, dtype=torch.float32)
        return flashinfer.sampling.top_p_sampling_from_probs(probs, p)
    elif p is None:
        probs = logits.softmax(dim=-1, dtype=torch.float32)
        return flashinfer.sampling.top_k_sampling_from_probs(probs, k)
    else:
        return flashinfer.sampling.top_k_top_p_sampling_from_logits(logits, k, p)
```

---

## 6. PENALTIES

**File:** `/vllm/v1/sample/ops/penalties.py` (lines 1-44)

V1 penalty application (calls shared V0 logic):

```python
def apply_all_penalties(logits, prompt_token_ids, presence_penalties,
                        frequency_penalties, repetition_penalties,
                        output_token_ids):
    output_tokens_t = _convert_to_tensors(output_token_ids, vocab_size, device)
    return apply_penalties(logits, prompt_token_ids, output_tokens_t,
                          presence_penalties, frequency_penalties,
                          repetition_penalties)
```

**Shared implementation:** `/vllm/model_executor/layers/utils.py` (lines 30-63)
- Uses `apply_repetition_penalties()` CUDA kernel
- Applies frequency & presence penalties via tensor operations

---

## 7. CUDA KERNELS

**File:** `/csrc/sampler.cu` (87 lines total)

**Kernel:** `apply_repetition_penalties_kernel()` (lines 14-44)
- Per-sequence, per-token repetition penalty application
- Uses block = (num_seqs, tile_num), thread = min(tile_size, 1024)
- Updates logits in-place:
  - If logit > 0: `logit /= penalty`
  - If logit ≤ 0: `logit *= penalty`

---

## 8. DATA FLOW: REQUEST → SAMPLING

```
Request with SamplingParams
  ↓
SequenceGroupMetadata
  ↓
_prepare_seq_groups() in SamplingMetadata.prepare()
  ├─ Creates SequenceGroupToSample objects
  ├─ Extracts selected_token_indices
  └─ Categorizes by SamplingType (GREEDY, RANDOM, RANDOM_SEED)
  ↓
SamplingTensors.from_sampling_metadata()
  ├─ Extracts per-sequence params (temperature, top_k, top_p, min_p, penalties)
  ├─ Detects if top-p/top-k needed (do_top_p_top_k flag)
  ├─ Detects if min_p needed (do_min_p flag)
  └─ Creates pinned tensors for H2D transfer
  ↓
Sampler.forward(logits, sampling_metadata)
  ├─ Apply min_tokens penalty (set stop tokens to -∞)
  ├─ Apply penalties (presence, frequency, repetition)
  ├─ Temperature scaling
  ├─ Top-k/Top-p filtering
  ├─ Min-p filtering
  ├─ Softmax → probabilities
  ├─ Sample tokens (greedy, multinomial, or FlashInfer)
  └─ Compute logprobs if requested
  ↓
SamplerOutput with sampled tokens + logprobs
```

---

## 9. KEY IMPLEMENTATION DETAILS

### Temperature Scaling
- **Location:** `/vllm/model_executor/layers/sampler.py:282`, `/vllm/v1/sample/sampler.py:112`
- In-place: `logits.div_(temperatures.unsqueeze(dim=1))`
- Greedy sampling: temperature = 0 → sets to 1 to avoid division by zero

### Top-P Implementation
- **Cumulative probability threshold:** Tokens included if cumsum(prob) ≤ 1 - top_p
- **Always include:** Last token forced to be included (no empty set)
- **Applied after top-k** (if both specified)

### Top-K Implementation
- Efficient: Only sorts when top-p is also needed
- Top-k-only optimization: Uses `topk()` instead of `sort()`
- Handles edge case: k ≥ vocab_size (no filtering)

### Min-P Implementation
- **Formula:** `min_threshold = min_p * max(probabilities)`
- Tokens removed if: `prob < scaled_min_p`
- **Location:** `/vllm/model_executor/layers/sampler.py:427-441`

### Penalty Application
- **Repetition:** Via CUDA kernel, scales by penalty factor
- **Frequency:** Linear subtraction: `logits -= freq_penalty * occurrence_count`
- **Presence:** Binary subtraction: `logits -= presence_penalty * (token_in_output ? 1 : 0)`

### Greedy Sampling
- **Logic:** Take argmax of logits/probabilities
- **Applied when:** temperature < 1e-5

### Random Sampling
- **Method:** Exponential variate trick (avoids CPU-GPU sync)
- **Seed support:** Per-request generators via torch.Generator
- **Multinomial avoided:** Because it causes CPU-GPU synchronization

### FlashInfer Integration (V1)
- **When enabled:** `envs.VLLM_USE_FLASHINFER_SAMPLER is not False`
- **Requires:** FlashInfer >= 0.2.3
- **Limitation:** No per-request generators (all requests must use same RNG)
- **Functions:** `top_k_sampling`, `top_p_sampling`, `top_k_top_p_sampling` from logits/probs

---

## 10. KEY FILE LOCATIONS SUMMARY

| Component | File Path | Line Range |
|-----------|-----------|-----------|
| SamplingParams definition | `vllm/sampling_params.py` | 118-603 |
| SequenceGroupToSample | `vllm/model_executor/sampling_metadata.py` | 19-76 |
| SamplingMetadata | `vllm/model_executor/sampling_metadata.py` | 97-196 |
| SamplingTensors | `vllm/model_executor/sampling_metadata.py` | 372-597 |
| V0 Sampler forward | `vllm/model_executor/layers/sampler.py` | 225-334 |
| V0 Top-K/Top-P | `vllm/model_executor/layers/sampler.py` | 398-424 |
| V0 Min-P | `vllm/model_executor/layers/sampler.py` | 427-441 |
| Penalties | `vllm/model_executor/layers/utils.py` | 30-63 |
| V1 Sampler forward | `vllm/v1/sample/sampler.py` | 24-76 |
| V1 SamplingMetadata | `vllm/v1/sample/metadata.py` | 1-44 |
| V1 TopKTopPSampler | `vllm/v1/sample/ops/topk_topp_sampler.py` | 22-297 |
| V1 Penalties | `vllm/v1/sample/ops/penalties.py` | 1-44 |
| CUDA Repetition Penalty | `csrc/sampler.cu` | 14-87 |
| Test Cases | `tests/samplers/test_sampler.py` | 565-642 |

---

This comprehensive exploration reveals that vLLM has two major sampling pipelines (V0 and V1), with V1 being more modular and featuring better FlashInfer integration. The core filtering logic for top-p, top-k, and min-p is implemented in pure PyTorch on both CPU and GPU, with optional CUDA acceleration via FlashInfer kernels.

### B. SamplingMetadata (lines 97-196)
Main metadata structure passed to sampler:
- `seq_groups: list[SequenceGroupToSample]` - batched sequences

- `categorized_sample_indices: dict[SamplingType, torch.Tensor]` - indices per sampling type

Tensors extracted from SamplingParams for GPU:
- `temperatures: torch.Tensor` - per-sequence temperature
- `top_ps: torch.Tensor` - per-sequence top-p values
- `top_ks: torch.Tensor` - per-sequence top-k values
- **`min_ps: torch.Tensor`** - per-sequence min-p values (line 379)
- `presence_penalties, frequency_penalties, repetition_penalties: torch.Tensor`
- `prompt_tokens, output_tokens: torch.Tensor` - for penalty calculation

**Construction method:** `SamplingTensors.from_sampling_metadata()` (lines 386-495)
- Extracts sampling params from each seq_group
- Detects if top-p/top-k is needed (lines 426-430)
- Detects if min-p is needed (line 429): `do_min_p = min_p > _SAMPLING_EPS`
- Creates per-sequence tensors pinned in CPU memory for async H2D transfer

## 3. CORE FILTERING LOGIC - V0 (Legacy) Sampler

**File:** `/vllm/model_executor/layers/sampler.py` (lines 1-1500+)

### A. Main Sampler Forward Pass (lines 225-334)
```python
def forward(logits, sampling_metadata):
    logits = _apply_min_tokens_penalty(logits, sampling_metadata)  # Line 269
    
    # 1. Apply penalties (presence, frequency, repetition)
    if do_penalties:
        logits = apply_penalties(logits, ...)  # Lines 272-277
    
    # 2. Temperature scaling
    logits.div_(temperatures)  # Line 282
    
    # 3. TOP-K/TOP-P filtering
    if do_top_p_top_k and flashinfer_sampling is None:
        logits = _apply_top_k_top_p(logits, top_ps, top_ks)  # Lines 284-286
    
    # 4. MIN-P filtering
    if do_min_p:
        logits = _apply_min_p(logits, min_ps)  # Lines 288-289
    
    # 5. Compute probabilities
    probs = softmax(logits)  # Line 293
    logprobs = log_softmax(logits)  # Line 295
    
    # 6. Sample from distribution
    sample_results = _sample(probs, logprobs, ...)  # Lines 298-305
```

### B. Top-K/Top-P Filtering (lines 398-424)

**Algorithm:**
1. Sort logits in descending order (line 403)
2. **Top-K:** Keep only top-k values, set others to -∞ (lines 406-410)
3. **Top-P:** Keep tokens until cumulative probability ≤ 1-p (lines 413-418)
4. Re-scatter back to original positions (lines 421-423)

```python
def _apply_top_k_top_p(logits, p, k):
    logits_sort, logits_idx = logits.sort(dim=-1, descending=False)
    
    # Top-k mask
    top_k_mask = logits_sort.size(1) - k.to(torch.long)
    top_k_mask = logits_sort.gather(1, top_k_mask.unsqueeze(dim=1))
    top_k_mask = logits_sort < top_k_mask
    logits_sort.masked_fill_(top_k_mask, -float("inf"))
    
    # Top-p mask (on softmaxed probs)
    probs_sort = logits_sort.softmax(dim=-1)
    probs_sum = probs_sort.cumsum(dim=-1)
    top_p_mask = probs_sum <= 1 - p.unsqueeze(dim=1)
    top_p_mask[:, -1] = False  # Ensure at least 1 token
    logits_sort.masked_fill_(top_p_mask, -float("inf"))
    
    # Scatter back
    logits = torch.empty_like(logits_sort).scatter_(
        dim=-1, index=logits_idx, src=logits_sort)
    return logits
```

### C. Min-P Filtering (lines 427-441)

**Algorithm:** Filter tokens below `min_p * max_probability`

```python
def _apply_min_p(logits, min_p):
    probs = torch.softmax(logits, dim=-1)
    top_probs, _ = probs.max(dim=-1, keepdim=True)
    scaled_min_p = min_p.unsqueeze_(dim=1) * top_probs  # Scale by max prob
    tokens_to_remove = probs < scaled_min_p
    logits = logits.masked_fill_(tokens_to_remove, -float("inf"))
    return logits
```

**Key insight:** min_p is relative to the maximum probability in the distribution.

### D. Penalty Application (lines 351-395)

Via `apply_penalties()` in `/vllm/model_executor/layers/utils.py` (lines 30-63):
- Computes binary masks for prompt/output tokens
- Applies repetition penalty via CUDA kernel `apply_repetition_penalties`
- Applies frequency penalty: `logits -= frequency_penalties * output_bin_counts`
- Applies presence penalty: `logits -= presence_penalties * output_mask`

## 4. V1 (NEW) SAMPLER WITH FLASHINFER INTEGRATION

**File:** `/vllm/v1/sample/sampler.py` (lines 1-200+)

### A. V1 Forward Pass (lines 24-76)

More modular design with separate concerns:

```python
def forward(logits, sampling_metadata):
    # 1. Allowed token filtering
    logits = apply_allowed_token_ids(logits, metadata)
    
    # 2. Bad words exclusion
    logits = apply_bad_words(logits, metadata)
    
    # 3. Non-argmax-invariant processors
    for processor in logitsprocs.non_argmax_invariant:
        logits = processor.apply(logits)
    
    # 4. Penalties (min_tokens, freq, presence, repetition)
    logits = apply_penalties(logits, metadata)
    
    # 5. Sample (handles temperature, top-k, top-p internally)
    sampled = sample(logits, metadata)
    
    # 6. Compute logprobs if requested
    logprobs_tensors = gather_logprobs(raw_logprobs, ...)
```


## 5. TOP-K/TOP-P IMPLEMENTATIONS - V1

**File:** `/vllm/v1/sample/ops/topk_topp_sampler.py` (lines 1-297)

### A. TopKTopPSampler Class (lines 22-119)

**Three implementations based on platform:**

1. **CUDA + FlashInfer (lines 85-107):** `forward_cuda()`
   - Uses FlashInfer library for optimized GPU sampling
   - Falls back to native if generators provided (per-request seeds)
   
2. **CPU/Native PyTorch (lines 69-83):** `forward_native()`
   - Pure PyTorch implementation
   
3. **TPU (lines 109-118):** `forward_tpu()`
   - TPU-optimized algorithm avoiding scatter operations

### B. Native Top-K/Top-P (lines 168-208)

```python
def apply_top_k_top_p(logits, k, p):
    logits_sort, logits_idx = logits.sort(dim=-1, descending=False)
    
    if k is not None:
        top_k_mask = logits_sort.size(1) - k.to(torch.long)
        top_k_mask = logits_sort.gather(1, top_k_mask.unsqueeze(dim=1))
        top_k_mask = logits_sort < top_k_mask
        logits_sort.masked_fill_(top_k_mask, -float("inf"))
    
    if p is not None:
        probs_sort = logits_sort.softmax(dim=-1)
        probs_sum = torch.cumsum(probs_sort, dim=-1, out=probs_sort)
        top_p_mask = probs_sum <= 1 - p.unsqueeze(dim=1)
        top_p_mask[:, -1] = False  # At least one token
        logits_sort.masked_fill_(top_p_mask, -float("inf"))
    
    logits = logits_sort.scatter(dim=-1, index=logits_idx, src=logits_sort)
    return logits
```

### C. Top-K Only Optimization (lines 211-233)

For top-k without top-p, avoids full sort:

```python
def apply_top_k_only(logits, k):
    no_top_k_mask = k == logits.shape[1]
    k = k.masked_fill(no_top_k_mask, 1)
    max_top_k = k.max()
    k_index = k.sub_(1).unsqueeze(1)
    top_k_mask = logits.topk(max_top_k, dim=1).values.gather(1, k_index.long())
    top_k_mask.masked_fill_(no_top_k_mask.unsqueeze(1), -float("inf"))
    logits.masked_fill_(logits < top_k_mask, -float("inf"))
    return logits
```

### D. TPU-Optimized Top-K/Top-P (lines 121-165)

Uses cumulative probability thresholding without scatter:

```python
def apply_top_k_top_p_tpu(logits, k, p):
    probs = logits.softmax(dim=-1)
    probs_sort, _ = probs.sort(dim=-1, descending=False)
    
    if k is not None:
        top_k_count = probs_sort.size(1) - k.to(torch.long)
        top_k_count = top_k_count.unsqueeze(dim=1)
        top_k_cutoff = probs_sort.gather(-1, top_k_count)
        no_top_k_mask = (k == logits.shape[1]).unsqueeze(dim=1)
        top_k_cutoff.masked_fill_(no_top_k_mask, -float("inf"))
        elements_to_discard = probs < top_k_cutoff
        logits.masked_fill_(elements_to_discard, -float("inf"))
    
    if p is not None:
        cumprob = torch.cumsum(probs_sort, dim=-1)
        top_p_mask = cumprob <= 1 - p.unsqueeze(dim=1)
        top_p_mask[:, -1] = False
        top_p_count = top_p_mask.sum(dim=-1).unsqueeze(1)
        top_p_cutoff = probs_sort.gather(-1, top_p_count)
        elements_to_discard = probs < top_p_cutoff
        logits.masked_fill_(elements_to_discard, -float("inf"))
    
    return logits
```

### E. Random Sampling (lines 236-257)

Uses exponential variate trick to avoid CPU-GPU sync:

```python
def random_sample(probs, generators):
    q = torch.empty_like(probs)
    if len(generators) != probs.shape[0]:
        q.exponential_()  # All requests without seeds
    if generators:
        for i, generator in generators.items():
            q[i].exponential_(generator=generator)  # Per-request seeds
    return probs.div_(q).argmax(dim=-1).view(-1)
```

### F. FlashInfer Integration (lines 260-296)

```python
def flashinfer_sample(logits, k, p, generators):
    # Uses FlashInfer CUDA kernels for fast sampling
    if k is None:
        probs = logits.softmax(dim=-1, dtype=torch.float32)
        return flashinfer.sampling.top_p_sampling_from_probs(probs, p)
    elif p is None:
        probs = logits.softmax(dim=-1, dtype=torch.float32)
        return flashinfer.sampling.top_k_sampling_from_probs(probs, k)
    else:
        return flashinfer.sampling.top_k_top_p_sampling_from_logits(logits, k, p)
```

## 6. PENALTIES

**File:** `/vllm/v1/sample/ops/penalties.py` (lines 1-44)

V1 penalty application (calls shared V0 logic):

```python
def apply_all_penalties(logits, prompt_token_ids, presence_penalties,
                        frequency_penalties, repetition_penalties,
                        output_token_ids):
    output_tokens_t = _convert_to_tensors(output_token_ids, vocab_size, device)
    return apply_penalties(logits, prompt_token_ids, output_tokens_t,
                          presence_penalties, frequency_penalties,
                          repetition_penalties)
```

**Shared implementation:** `/vllm/model_executor/layers/utils.py` (lines 30-63)
- Uses `apply_repetition_penalties()` CUDA kernel
- Applies frequency & presence penalties via tensor operations

---

## 7. CUDA KERNELS

**File:** `/csrc/sampler.cu` (87 lines total)

**Kernel:** `apply_repetition_penalties_kernel()` (lines 14-44)
- Per-sequence, per-token repetition penalty application
- Uses block = (num_seqs, tile_num), thread = min(tile_size, 1024)
- Updates logits in-place:
  - If logit > 0: `logit /= penalty`
  - If logit ≤ 0: `logit *= penalty`

---

## 8. DATA FLOW: REQUEST → SAMPLING

```
Request with SamplingParams
  ↓
SequenceGroupMetadata
  ↓
_prepare_seq_groups() in SamplingMetadata.prepare()
  ├─ Creates SequenceGroupToSample objects
  ├─ Extracts selected_token_indices
  └─ Categorizes by SamplingType (GREEDY, RANDOM, RANDOM_SEED)
  ↓
SamplingTensors.from_sampling_metadata()
  ├─ Extracts per-sequence params (temperature, top_k, top_p, min_p, penalties)
  ├─ Detects if top-p/top-k needed (do_top_p_top_k flag)
  ├─ Detects if min_p needed (do_min_p flag)
  └─ Creates pinned tensors for H2D transfer
  ↓
Sampler.forward(logits, sampling_metadata)
  ├─ Apply min_tokens penalty (set stop tokens to -∞)
  ├─ Apply penalties (presence, frequency, repetition)
  ├─ Temperature scaling
  ├─ Top-k/Top-p filtering
  ├─ Min-p filtering
  ├─ Softmax → probabilities
  ├─ Sample tokens (greedy, multinomial, or FlashInfer)
  └─ Compute logprobs if requested
  ↓
SamplerOutput with sampled tokens + logprobs
```

---

## 9. KEY IMPLEMENTATION DETAILS

### Temperature Scaling
- **Location:** `/vllm/model_executor/layers/sampler.py:282`, `/vllm/v1/sample/sampler.py:112`
- In-place: `logits.div_(temperatures.unsqueeze(dim=1))`
- Greedy sampling: temperature = 0 → sets to 1 to avoid division by zero

### Top-P Implementation
- **Cumulative probability threshold:** Tokens included if cumsum(prob) ≤ 1 - top_p
- **Always include:** Last token forced to be included (no empty set)
- **Applied after top-k** (if both specified)

### Top-K Implementation
- Efficient: Only sorts when top-p is also needed
- Top-k-only optimization: Uses `topk()` instead of `sort()`
- Handles edge case: k ≥ vocab_size (no filtering)

### Min-P Implementation
- **Formula:** `min_threshold = min_p * max(probabilities)`
- Tokens removed if: `prob < scaled_min_p`
- **Location:** `/vllm/model_executor/layers/sampler.py:427-441`

### Penalty Application
- **Repetition:** Via CUDA kernel, scales by penalty factor
- **Frequency:** Linear subtraction: `logits -= freq_penalty * occurrence_count`
- **Presence:** Binary subtraction: `logits -= presence_penalty * (token_in_output ? 1 : 0)`

### Greedy Sampling
- **Logic:** Take argmax of logits/probabilities
- **Applied when:** temperature < 1e-5

### Random Sampling
- **Method:** Exponential variate trick (avoids CPU-GPU sync)
- **Seed support:** Per-request generators via torch.Generator
- **Multinomial avoided:** Because it causes CPU-GPU synchronization

### FlashInfer Integration (V1)
- **When enabled:** `envs.VLLM_USE_FLASHINFER_SAMPLER is not False`
- **Requires:** FlashInfer >= 0.2.3
- **Limitation:** No per-request generators (all requests must use same RNG)
- **Functions:** `top_k_sampling`, `top_p_sampling`, `top_k_top_p_sampling` from logits/probs

---

## 10. KEY FILE LOCATIONS SUMMARY

| Component | File Path | Line Range |
|-----------|-----------|-----------|
| SamplingParams definition | `vllm/sampling_params.py` | 118-603 |
| SequenceGroupToSample | `vllm/model_executor/sampling_metadata.py` | 19-76 |
| SamplingMetadata | `vllm/model_executor/sampling_metadata.py` | 97-196 |
| SamplingTensors | `vllm/model_executor/sampling_metadata.py` | 372-597 |
| V0 Sampler forward | `vllm/model_executor/layers/sampler.py` | 225-334 |
| V0 Top-K/Top-P | `vllm/model_executor/layers/sampler.py` | 398-424 |
| V0 Min-P | `vllm/model_executor/layers/sampler.py` | 427-441 |
| Penalties | `vllm/model_executor/layers/utils.py` | 30-63 |
| V1 Sampler forward | `vllm/v1/sample/sampler.py` | 24-76 |
| V1 SamplingMetadata | `vllm/v1/sample/metadata.py` | 1-44 |
| V1 TopKTopPSampler | `vllm/v1/sample/ops/topk_topp_sampler.py` | 22-297 |
| V1 Penalties | `vllm/v1/sample/ops/penalties.py` | 1-44 |
| CUDA Repetition Penalty | `csrc/sampler.cu` | 14-87 |
| Test Cases | `tests/samplers/test_sampler.py` | 565-642 |

---

This comprehensive exploration reveals that vLLM has two major sampling pipelines (V0 and V1), with V1 being more modular and featuring better FlashInfer integration. The core filtering logic for top-p, top-k, and min-p is implemented in pure PyTorch on both CPU and GPU, with optional CUDA acceleration via FlashInfer kernels.

def get_diff_sampling_param(self) -> dict[str, Any]:
        """
        This method returns a dictionary containing the parameters
        that differ from the default sampling parameters. If
        `generation_config` is `"vllm"`, an empty dictionary is returned.

        Returns:
            dict[str, Any]: A dictionary with the differing sampling
            parameters, if `generation_config` is `"vllm"` an empty dictionary.
        """
        if self.generation_config == "vllm":
            config = {}
        else:
            config = self.try_get_generation_config()

        # Overriding with given generation config
        config.update(self.override_generation_config)

        available_params = [
            "repetition_penalty",
            "temperature",
            "top_k",
            "top_p",
            "min_p",
            "max_new_tokens",
        ]
        if any(p in config for p in available_params):
            diff_sampling_param = {
                p: config.get(p)
                for p in available_params if config.get(p) is not None
            }
